name: dnfile
documentation:
# dnfile

```{image} https://github.com/malwarefrank/dnfile/actions/workflows/lint.yml/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/malwarefrank/dnfile/actions/workflows/lint.yml
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/dnfile.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/dnfile
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/dnfile
:target: https://pypistats.org/packages/dnfile
```

Parse .NET executable files.

- Free software: MIT license

## Features

- Parse as much as we can, even if the file is partially malformed.
- Easy to use. Developed with IDE autocompletion in mind.

## Quick Start

```shell
pip install dnfile
```

Then create a simple program that loads a .NET binary, parses it, and displays
information about the streams and Metadata Tables.

```python
import sys
import dnfile

filepath = sys.argv[1]

pe = dnfile.dnPE(filepath)
pe.print_info()
```

Everything is an object, and raw structure values are stored in an object's "struct"
attribute. The CLR directory entry object is accessible from the "net"
attribute of a dnPE object.

```python
import dnfile
import hashlib

pe = dnfile.dnPE(FILEPATH)

# access the directory entry raw structure values
pe.net.struct

# access the metadata raw structure values
pe.net.metadata.struct

# access the streams
for s in pe.net.metadata.streams_list:
    if isinstance(s, dnfile.stream.MetaDataTables):
        # how many Metadata tables are defined in the binary?
        num_of_tables = len(s.tables_list)

# the last Metadata tables stream can also be accessed by a shortcut
num_of_tables = len(pe.net.mdtables.tables_list)

# create a set to hold the hashes of all resources
res_hash = set()
# access the resources
for r in pe.net.resources:
    # if resource data is a simple byte stream
    if isinstance(r.data, bytes):
        # hash it and add the hash to the set
        res_hash.add(hashlib.sha256(r.data).hexdigest())
    # if resource data is a ResourceSet, a dotnet-specific datatype
    elif isinstance(r.data, dnfile.resource.ResourceSet):
        # if there are no entries
        if not r.data.entries:
            # skip it
            continue
        # for each entry in the ResourceSet
        for entry in r.data.entries:
            # if it has data
            if entry.data:
                # hash it and add the hash to the set
                res_hash.add(hashlib.sha256(entry.data).hexdigest())
```

## TODO

- more tests
- Documentation on readthedocs

## Credits

This package was created with [Cookiecutter] and the [audreyr/cookiecutter-pypackage] project template.

# History

## 0.15.1 (2024)

- BUGFIX: read_compressed_int() now returns None on invalid data len instead of IndexError
- BUGFIX: when parsing resources, ignore DateTimeKind bits of a serialized System.DateTime
- EXAMPLE: Iterate guids

## 0.15.0 (2024)

- BREAKING CHANGE: each heap stream's .get() returns a HeapItem instead of bytes
- FEATURE: All HeapItem objects include the RVA of where they were retrieved
- FEATURE: HeapItemBinary objects allow easy access to interpreted item size (CompressedInt)
- FEATURE: HeapItemString and UserString allow easy access to raw bytes and interpreted value
- improvements to pypi publishing and tox testing

## 0.14.1 (2023)

- fix github workflow

## 0.14.0 (2023)

- BREAKING CHANGE: Minimum required Python version is now 3.8
- BUGFIX: ValueError fired before UnicodeDecodeError when parsing assembly resources
- BUGFIX: mdtable row run-lists of size one were being ignored
- BUGFIX: some struct file offsets were RVA values
- FEATURE: Add `clr_lazy_load` option for lazy loading Metadata tables and assembly resources
- move from legacy setup.py to pyproject.toml and tox
- bump dev dependencies: mypy and isort
- update tests and examples
- update README badge to use download statistics from pypistats

## 0.13.0 (2022)

- BREAKING CHANGE: rename GenericMethod mdtable to MethodSpec per ECMA 335
- parse more resources, even if there are exceptions

## 0.12.0 (2022)

- FEATURE: parse `#Schema` stream as MetaDataTables
- BUGFIX: MDTableRow off-by-one for end of run
- BUGFIX: MethodSemanticsRow typo list of tables for the Method Index
- more test data

## 0.11.0 (2022)

- FEATURE: access .NET resources (not same as PE resources!) by a shortcut
- BUGFIX: dnstrings example
- more attributes default to None
- update dev dependencies
- remove some warnings

## 0.10.0 (2022)

- BREAKING CHANGE: structure attributes no longer exist by default
- BREAKING CHANGE: objects' attributes always exist, but may be None
- BUGFIX: use last stream if multiple of same name
- CI: added mypy type checking
- when duplicate stream names, behave like runtime and use last one for shortcuts
- add user_strings shortcut
- able to access MetaDataTables like a 0-based list, with square brackets
- added use of logging module for warnings
- better type hints for IDEs
- more better source comments
- more tests

## 0.9.0 (2021)

- bugfix: row indices parsed in structures are one-based, not zero-based
- bugfix: TypeDefRow was not parsing Extends coded index
- bugfix: incorrect BLOBS_MASK and add EXTRA_DATA skip if flag set
- added CI using github workflow
- added tests and submodule dnfile-testfiles
- added style consistency using pycodestyle and isort
- added more examples
- parse MetaData tables' list-type indexes into lists of MDTableRow objects

## 0.8.0 (2021)

- bugfix: Metadata Table indexes (i.e. indexes into other tables) were off by one

## 0.7.1 (2021)

- bugfix: coded index always None

## 0.7.0 (2021)

- bugfix: improper data length check

## 0.6.0 (2021)

- bugfix: referenced wrong object
- parse utf-16 strings in #US stream

## 0.5.0 (2021-01-29)

- First release.

[audreyr/cookiecutter-pypackage]: https://github.com/audreyr/cookiecutter-pypackage
[cookiecutter]: https://github.com/audreyr/cookiecutter

modules.name: dnfile.utils
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: dnfile.utils.lru_cache
documentation: lru_cache with a shallow copy of the objects returned (list, dicts, ..)
we don't use deepcopy as it's _really_ slow and the data we retrieved using this is enough with copy.copy
taken from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/54909357/how-to-get-functools-lru-cache-to-return-new-instances

functions.name: dnfile.utils.read_compressed_int
documentation:
Given bytes, read a compressed integer per
spec ECMA-335 II.23.2 Blobs and signatures.
Returns tuple: value, number of bytes read or None on error.

functions.name: dnfile.utils.compress_int
documentation:
Given integer, return bytes representing a compressed integer per
spec ECMA-335 II.23.2 Blobs and signatures.

functions.name: dnfile.utils.num_bytes_to_struct_char
documentation:
Given number of bytes, return the struct char that can hold those bytes.
Returns None on invalid value.

For example,
    2 = H
    4 = I

classes.name: dnfile.utils.LazyList

methods.name: dnfile.utils.LazyList.__init__
documentation:
Create a lazily initialized list of the specified size.

`eval_func` is called with the index and value (or slice and list of values)
for items retrieved through `__getitem__` *every time they are accessed*.

methods.name: dnfile.utils.LazyList.eval_all
documentation: Ensure all items in the list are initialized by iterating through them.

methods.name: dnfile.utils.LazyList.truncate
documentation: Truncate the list without evaluating any items.

A List that transparently initializes each item as it is accessed.

Note that calling `str` or `repr` with an instance of this class will force
all items to be initialized.

methods.name: dnfile.dnPE.dump_info
documentation: Dump all the PE and CLR header information into human readable string.

methods.name: dnfile.dnPE.get_warnings
documentation: Returns a copy of the list of warning messages.

classes.name: dnfile.ClrMetaData

methods.name: dnfile.ClrMetaData.__init__
documentation:
Given a dnPE object, MetaData RVA and MetaData Size.
Raises dnFormatError if encounter problems parsing.
### MetaData section
dd    Signature
dw    MajorVersion
dw    MinorVersion
dd    Reserved
dd    Length
var   Version
dw    Flags
dw    NumberOfStreams
var   StreamHeaders

Holds CLR (.NET) MetaData.

struct:         IMAGE_CLR_METADATA structure
streams:        Dictionary to access streams by name (bytes)
streams_list:   List of streams in order of entry in header

classes.name: dnfile.ClrData

methods.name: dnfile.ClrData.__init__
documentation:
Given dnPE object, .NET header RVA and header size.
Raises dnFormatError if problems parsing.

methods.name: dnfile.ClrData._init_resources
documentation:
Parse and initialize assembly resources.

This is separate from `ClrData.__init__` to allow for mdtable lazy-loading since
parsing `ManifestResourceRow.Implementation` requires all tables to be loaded.

Holds CLR (.NET) header data.

struct:         IMAGE_NET_DIRECTORY structure
metadata:       ClrMetaData or None
strings:        stream.StringsHeap or None
user_strings:   stream.UserStringsHeap or None
guids:          stream.GuidHeap or None
blobs:          stream.BlobHeap or None
mdtables:       stream.MetaDataTables or None
Flags:          enums.ClrHeaderFlags or None

modules.name: dnfile.enums
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: dnfile.enums._getvars
documentation: Most developers may just use the Clr* classes to automatically parse the
flags defined in winsdk corhdr.h
The definitions in winsdk corhdr.h may be accesses through the Cor* classes.

classes.name: dnfile.enums.ClrMetaDataEnum
documentation: Base class for CorHdr.h metadata enumerations.

classes.name: dnfile.enums.ClrFlags
documentation:
Base class for CLR MetaData Tables' Flags.

When instantiated, this class takes a value and sets member vars to True according to IntEnum's in _masks and _flags.

Note that _flags are bitmasks that match on single bits, whereas _masks are enum values that match exact value.

:var corhdr_enum:   the class that defines values from winsdk corhdr.h, likely not needed by most developers.
:var _masks:        a dictionary that defines the masks and associated values (classes) to check and set if matching exactly.
:var _flags:        an iterable of classes defining bit flags to check and set if set.

methods.name: dnfile.enums.ClrHeaderFlags.__init__
documentation: Given a value, instantiates self with members set to True according to value.

classes.name: dnfile.enums.AssemblyHashAlgorithm
documentation:
Per Microsoft documentation, "Specifies all the hash algorithms used for hashing files and for generating the strong name."

REFERENCE:
    https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.configuration.assemblies.assemblyhashalgorithm?view=net-5.0

methods.name: dnfile.resource.ResourceTypeFactory.read_serialized_data
documentation:
Read a serialized data (compressed int size, followed by data of that many bytes).

Return the data (bytes) and number of bytes read (size + data).

methods.name: dnfile.resource.ResourceTypeFactory.type_str_to_type
documentation:
Given a type string, data buffer, and offset into that buffer.
Return a tuple of the raw data and the deserialized value.

methods.name: dnfile.resource.ResourceSet.read_serialized_data
documentation:
Read a serialized data (compressed int size, followed by data of that many bytes).

Return the data (bytes) and number of bytes read (size + data).

modules.name: dnfile.stream
documentation:
.NET Streams

REFERENCES

    https://www.ntcore.com/files/dotnetformat.htm
    https://referencesource.microsoft.com/System.AddIn/System/Addin/MiniReflection/MetadataReader/Metadata.cs.html#123
    ECMA-335 6th Edition, June 2012, Section II.24.2.4 #US and #Blob heaps

Copyright (c) 2020-2024 MalwareFrank
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: dnfile.stream.GenericStream
documentation: A generic CLR Stream of unknown type.

classes.name: dnfile.stream.HeapItemString
documentation:
A HeapItemString is a HeapItem with an encoding.  The .value member
is the decoded string or None if there was a UnicodeDecodeError.

A HeapItemString can be compared directly to a str.

classes.name: dnfile.stream.HeapItemBinary
documentation:
A HeapItemBinary is a HeapItem with an item_size.  The .item_size
is the parsed compressed integer at the RVA in the binary heap.
The .value is the bytes following the compressed integer.

A HeapItemBinary can be compared directly to a bytes object.

methods.name: dnfile.stream.StringsHeap.get_str
documentation:
Given an index (offset), read a null-terminated UTF-8 (or given encoding) string.
Returns None on error, or string, or bytes if as_bytes is True.

methods.name: dnfile.stream.StringsHeap.get
documentation:
Given an index (offset), read a null-terminated UTF-8 (or given encoding) string.
Returns a HeapItemString, or None on error.

classes.name: dnfile.stream.UserString
documentation:
The #US or UserStrings stream should contain UTF-16 strings.
Each entry in the stream includes a byte indicating whether
any Unicode characters require handling beyond that normally
provided for 8-bit encoding sets.

Reference ECMA-335, Partition II Section 24.2.4

classes.name: dnfile.stream.MetaDataTables

methods.name: dnfile.stream.MetaDataTables.parse
documentation:
this may raise an exception if the data cannot be parsed correctly.
however, `self` may still be partially initialized with *some* data.

Holds CLR (.NET) Metadata Tables.

struct:     the stream list entry
header:     IMAGE_CLR_METADATA_TABLES structure
tables:     dict of tables where table number is key and value is CLRMetaDataTable object
tables_list:            list of tables, in processing order
strings_offset_size:    number of bytes
guids_offset_size:      number of bytes
blobs_offset_size:      number of bytes

modules.name: dnfile.base
documentation:
.NET base classes

Copyright (c) 2020-2024 MalwareFrank
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: dnfile.base._row_class_struct_attrs
documentation:
Retrieve all possible attributes for a `MDTableRow` class,
along with their associated struct type.

Attributes are separated based on whether they can be loaded without data from
any other tables.
Computing this for each class takes some time, especially if it is done for every row,
but it *should* remain consistent for any given class and therefore can be cached.

methods.name: dnfile.base.ClrStream.parse
documentation:
Parse the stream.

NOTE: do not call until all streams have been initialized,
      since we may need info from other streams.

methods.name: dnfile.base.ClrStream.stream_table_entry_size
documentation: Returns the number of bytes occupied by this entry in the Streams table list.

methods.name: dnfile.base.ClrStream.sizeof
documentation: Return the size of this stream, in bytes.

methods.name: dnfile.base.ClrStream.get_file_offset
documentation:
Return the file offset of the given RVA within this stream.
If no RVA given, then return the file offset of this stream.

classes.name: dnfile.base.HeapItem

methods.name: dnfile.base.HeapItem.value_bytes
documentation:
Return the raw bytes underlying the interpreted value.

For the base HeapItem, this is the same as the raw_data.

methods.name: dnfile.base.HeapItem.raw_size
documentation:
Number of bytes read from the stream, including any header,
value, and footer.

methods.name: dnfile.base.HeapItem.raw_data
documentation:
The bytes read from the stream, including any header,
value, and footer

methods.name: dnfile.base.HeapItem.__eq__
documentation:
Two HeapItems are equal if their raw data is the same or their
interpreted values are the same and not Noney.

A HeapItem is equal to a bytes object if the HeapItem's value as bytes
is equal to the bytes object.

HeapItem is a base class for items retrieved from any of the
heap streams, for example #Strings, #US, #GUID, and #Blob.

It can be used to access the raw underlying data, the RVA
from which it was retrieved, an optional interpreted value,
and the bytes representation of the value.

Each heap stream .get() call returns a subclass with these
and optionally additional members.

classes.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow

methods.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow.__init__
documentation:
Given the tables' row counts and heap info.

Initialize the following attributes:
    row_size    The size, in bytes, of one row.  Calculated from
                 tables_rowcounts, heap info, and self._format
    struct      The class used to parse the data.

tables_rowcounts is indexed by table number.  The value is the row count, if it exists, or None.

methods.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow._compute_format
documentation:
Compute the structure format.
This will be passed to RowStruct instances to calcuate the row size and to parse a row.

This may raise an exception when the offsets to referenced streams is too large (>4 bytes).

methods.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow.set_data
documentation:
Parse the data and set struct for this row.

NOTE that the row is not fully parsed, and attributes not set, until
parse() is called after all tables have had parse_rows() called on them.

methods.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow.setup_lazy_load
documentation:
Mark this row for lazy-loading.

`full_loader` will be called if a property is requested that requires
loading all mdtables before it can be parsed.

methods.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow.__getattr__
documentation:
If this row is marked for lazy-loading, attempt to load the struct
that contains the requested property and try again.

If the requested property requires data from other
mdtables, all tables will be loaded.

methods.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow.parse
documentation:
Parse the row data and set object attributes.  Should only be called after all rows of all tables
have been initialized, i.e. parse_rows() has been called on each table in the tables list.

    next_row    the next row in the table, used for row lists (e.g. FieldList, MethodList)

methods.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow._clr_coded_index_struct_size
documentation:
Given table names and tag bits, checks for the max row count
size among the given tables and returns "H" if it will fit in
a word or "I" if not, assuming tag bits number of bits are used
by tag.

The returned character can be used in a Structure format or
passing to struct.pack()

class_variables.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow._struct_codedindexes
documentation: also CodedIndex subclass

class_variables.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow._struct_indexes
documentation: also Metadata table name

class_variables.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow._struct_flags
documentation: also ClrFlags subclass

class_variables.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow._struct_enums
documentation: also enum.IntEnum subclassA

class_variables.name: dnfile.base.MDTableRow._struct_lists
documentation: also Metadata table name

This is the base class for Metadata Tables' rows.

A Metadata Table row is a simple structure that holds the
fields and values.

classes.name: dnfile.base.MDTableIndex
documentation:
An index into a Metadata Table.

Attributes:
    table           Table object.
    row_index       Index number of the row.
    row             The referenced row.

classes.name: dnfile.base.CodedIndex
documentation:
Subclasses should be sure to set the following attributes:
  - tag_bits        Number of bits used to specify the table name index.
  - table_names     Candidate list of table names.

classes.name: dnfile.base.ClrMetaDataTable

methods.name: dnfile.base.ClrMetaDataTable.__init__
documentation:
Given the tables' row counts, sorted flag, and heap info.
Initialize the following attributes:
    num_rows    The number of rows, according to tables_rowscount.
    row_size    The size, in bytes, of one row.  Calculated from
                tables_info, tables_rowcounts, and self._format
    is_sorted   Whether the table is sorted, according to tables_info.
    rows        Initialized but not-yet-parsed list of rows.

methods.name: dnfile.base.ClrMetaDataTable.setup_lazy_load
documentation:
Mark this table for lazy-loading.

`full_loader` will be called if a row property is requested that requires
loading all mdtables before it can be parsed.

methods.name: dnfile.base.ClrMetaDataTable._lazy_parse_row
documentation:
If the row has not been loaded, return a new lazy-loaded row initialized
with the proper data for the row index.

methods.name: dnfile.base.ClrMetaDataTable._lazy_parse_rows
documentation: Convenience function to handle both indexing and slicing of the LazyList.

methods.name: dnfile.base.ClrMetaDataTable.parse_rows
documentation:
Given a byte sequence containing the rows, add data to each row in the
self.rows list.  Note that the rows have not been fully parsed until
parse() is called, which should not happen until all tables have been
initialized and parse_rows() called on each.

methods.name: dnfile.base.ClrMetaDataTable.parse
documentation:
Fully parse the table, resolving references to heaps, indexes into other
tables, and coded indexes into other tables.

NOTE: do not call until ALL tables have been initialized and parse_rows()
called on each.

methods.name: dnfile.base.ClrMetaDataTable.__len__
documentation:
the actual number of rows parsed,
as opposed to self.num_rows, which is the declared row count of the table

methods.name: dnfile.base.ClrMetaDataTable.get_with_row_index
documentation:
fetch the row with the given row index.
remember: row indices, at least those encoded within a .NET file, are 1-based.
so, you should prefer to use this method when you get a reference to a row.
use `__getitem__` when you want 0-based indexing.

An abstract class for Metadata tables.  Rows can be accessed
 directly like a list with bracket [] syntax.
Use `get_with_row_index` when you have a Rid/token/row_index,
 since these are 1-indexed.
Use bracket [] syntax when you want 0-indexing.

Subclasses should make sure to set the following attributes:
    number
    name
    _row_class

modules.name: dnfile.mdtable
documentation:
.NET Metadata Tables


REFERENCES

    https://www.ntcore.com/files/dotnetformat.htm
    https://referencesource.microsoft.com/System.AddIn/System/Addin/MiniReflection/MetadataReader/Metadata.cs.html#123
    ECMA-335, 6th Edition


Copyright (c) 2020-2022 MalwareFrank
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: dnfile.mdtable.checked_offset_format
documentation:
compute the format specifier needed for the given offset value.
raises an exception if the offset cannot be represented.
